summary: Finished matching the game-mode source file src/melee/gm/gm_1A45.c and promoted it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The source changes were small and centered on gm_801A4D34: adjusting local variable types/names, increasing PAD_STACK from 20 to 28, replacing a manual while loop counter with a for loop, and assigning maybe_gm_801A48A4 directly into temp_r25->unk_20 instead of using a temporary u64.

key_files.role: Matched source file; contains the gm_801A4D34 cleanup that enabled the file to become matching.
Build configuration updated to mark melee/gm/gm_1A45.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.

what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gm_1A45.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gm_1A45.c").
In gm_801A4D34, removed the u64 temp_ret temporary and assigned maybe_gm_801A48A4(temp_r25->unk_10) directly to temp_r25->unk_20.
Changed loop/index locals from s32 var_r26 and u8 temp_r3 to int i and int temp_r3.
Replaced a manual while loop with var_r26 initialization/increment with a for (i = 0; i < temp_r3; i++) loop.
Adjusted PAD_STACK from 20 to 28 in gm_801A4D34.
No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
smart_moves: The final match was achieved through minimal C-shape changes rather than broad restructuring, preserving the existing function logic.
Using a for loop with an int induction variable likely guided the compiler toward the intended register allocation and loop shape.
Removing the extra u64 temporary for maybe_gm_801A48A4 reduced unnecessary lifetime/register pressure and matched the target assignment form.
The PAD_STACK adjustment shows that stack padding was used precisely as a matching aid for this function.
decomp_lessons: When a file is nearly matching, small differences in local variable type width, loop spelling, and temporary lifetimes can be enough to close the remaining gap.
For loops and while loops are not interchangeable for matching purposes; choose the spelling that produces the target induction-variable codegen.
Avoid introducing named temporaries when the target code appears to store a call result directly into a struct field.
After a source file is verified matching, remember to update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching so the build system tracks the file correctly.
naming_conventions: Existing unknown struct and field names were retained, including gm_80479D58_t and fields such as unk_10, unk_11, unk_12, unk_13, unk_20, and unk_C.
Temporary decomp variable names follow common placeholder style such as temp_r25 and temp_r3 when semantic names are not yet known.
The loop counter was simplified to a generic local name i after changing the loop to for-loop form.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Increase PAD_STACK from 20 to 28 to align the generated stack frame with the target.
Change local integer widths to int where narrower or explicit signed types caused nonmatching codegen.
Use direct struct-field assignment from maybe_gm_801A48A4 instead of routing through a u64 temporary.
Prefer for-loop syntax when it better matches target loop initialization, comparison, and increment placement.
Once verified, flip the configure.py object status from NonMatching to Matching.
follow_up_queries: Find other gm files still marked NonMatching in configure.py near gm_1A45.c.
Search for PAD_STACK adjustments in recently matched game-mode files.
Search for maybe_gm_801A48A4 and gm_80479D58 usages to improve naming of unknown fields.
Compare loop-shape fixes in other PRs that converted while loops to for loops for matching.
